text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's internal structure without changing what it does. Think of it like reorganizing a messy kitchen: you’re not cooking a different meal, but you’re making the workspace cleaner and more efficient. By renaming confusing variables, breaking long functions into smaller ones, or simplifying complex logic, you make the code easier to read and maintain. This reduces technical debt—the extra work that builds up from quick, disorganized solutions. Regular refactoring keeps your codebase healthy, helping you and your team work faster and with fewer bugs in the long run.
